stem: Which of the following is a root of \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) = 0 \)?
solution: The cubic factors as \( (x - ([[r1]]))(x - ([[r2]]))(x - ([[r3]])) \), so its roots are \( [[r1]], [[r2]], [[r3]] \). One of these is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is one more than a root; substituting it does not give zero.
This negates a root; sign matters, and the negated value is not a root here.
This sums two of the roots, which is not itself a root.
This value is well outside the range of the roots, so it cannot be a zero.
This value is well outside the range of the roots, so it cannot be a zero.
This value is well outside the range of the roots, so it cannot be a zero.

stem: Which of the following is a root of \( ([[c3]])x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) = 0 \)?
solution: The cubic factors as \( ([[m]]x - ([[n]]))(x - ([[r1]]))(x - ([[r2]])) \), so by the Rational Root Theorem its roots are \( \dfrac{[[n]]}{[[m]]} \), \( [[r1]] \), and \( [[r2]] \). One of these is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the numerator \( n \) alone; the actual rational root is \( n/m \), not \( n \) itself.
This is off by one from the true rational root \( n/m \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign for the rational root.
This is one more than a genuine integer root.
This is one more than a genuine integer root.
This value is well outside the range of the roots, so it cannot be a zero.

stem: The equation \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) = 0 \) has a repeated (double) root and one other, different root. What is the SUM of its DISTINCT real zeros (each counted only once)?
solution: The cubic factors as \( (x-([[r]]))^{2}(x-([[s]])) \), so its zeros are \( [[r]] \) (a double root) and \( [[s]] \). Counting each distinct zero only once, the sum is \( [[r]] + [[s]] = [[answer]] \). (This differs from the full Vieta sum \( 2([[r]]) + [[s]] \), which counts the double root twice.)
distractor_rationales: This is the full Vieta sum of the roots WITH multiplicity, counting the double root twice.
This mistakenly doubles the simple root \( s \) instead of the repeated root \( r \).
This has the correct magnitude as the Vieta sum but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct sum of distinct zeros.
This is off by one from the correct sum of distinct zeros.
This is off by two from the correct sum of distinct zeros.

stem: Which of the following is NOT a root of \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) = 0 \)?
solution: The cubic factors as \( (x-([[r1]]))(x-([[r2]]))(x-([[r3]])) \), so its only roots are \( [[r1]], [[r2]], [[r3]] \). The value \( [[m]] \) is none of these, so it is NOT a root.
distractor_rationales: This IS one of the genuine roots of the cubic, so it is a poor choice for 'NOT a root'.
This IS one of the genuine roots of the cubic, so it is a poor choice for 'NOT a root'.
This IS one of the genuine roots of the cubic, so it is a poor choice for 'NOT a root'.
This is a nearby number chosen to look plausible, but it is off by one from the intended non-root value.
This is a nearby number chosen to look plausible, but it is off by one from the intended non-root value.
This is a nearby number chosen to look plausible, but it is off by two from the intended non-root value.
